headline: Scientists discover 31 new ocean species off Brazil in two weeks
meta_description: Researchers exploring deep waters off Brazil found 31 previously unknown species, including nine jellyfish and a fast-moving deep sea worm.
summary_short: In just two weeks of exploration off Brazil's coast, scientists uncovered 31 new species never before documented, expanding our understanding of ocean life.
body:
Scientists exploring the deep ocean off Brazil have made a remarkable discovery: 31 species previously unknown to science, all found in just two weeks of research.

The haul includes nine new jellyfish species, seven comb jellies, and a fast-moving deep sea worm—each addition a window into ecosystems that remain largely unmapped. The sheer speed of discovery underscores how much of our ocean remains unexplored, and how readily new life forms reveal themselves when scientists venture into the depths.

Findings like these matter beyond the thrill of discovery. Each new species catalogued strengthens our understanding of marine biodiversity, offering clues about ocean health, evolutionary adaptation, and the intricate webs that sustain life in Earth's final frontier. In two weeks off one stretch of Brazilian coast, science expanded our known world.
quick_facts: 31 new species discovered off Brazil's coast
Found in just two weeks of exploration
Seven comb jellies among the discoveries
Fast-moving deep sea worm also documented
